version: 1
---

# {{Project Name}} Vision

<!-- The vision is the durable product charter. It should be short enough for a
     new human contributor or AI session to read before doing work. It is not a
     module spec and should not contain implementation details. -->

## Job To Be Done

<!-- HUMAN - Canonical when/want/so statement for the project.

     Example:
     When <primary user> is <situation>, they want <capability>, so they can
     <outcome>. -->

## Users And Stakeholders

<!-- HUMAN - Who the project serves and who can constrain it. Include primary
     users, secondary users, operators, maintainers, and external stakeholders. -->

## Success Shape

<!-- HUMAN - What must be true for the project to be considered successful?
     Prefer concrete outcomes over feature lists. -->

## Non-Goals

<!-- HUMAN - What this project deliberately does not do. Each non-goal should
     prevent a plausible future expansion from leaking into the wrong scope. -->

## Constraints

<!-- HUMAN - Durable constraints: regulatory, data, cost, latency, reliability,
     portability, human-review, or deployment constraints. -->

## Operating Principles

<!-- HUMAN - Project-specific principles that complement PRINCIPLES.md. Keep
     this short. Long engineering philosophy belongs in PRINCIPLES.md. -->

## Milestones

<!-- HUMAN - Optional. Major phases or confidence checkpoints. Avoid turning
     this into an implementation plan. -->

## Review Cadence

<!-- HUMAN - When should this vision be revisited? State a cadence or trigger. -->
